name: Paramedic / EMT
clinical_context: Works 24-on/48-off or 12h rotating shifts with frequent interrupted sleep during on-call periods. Physical demands of patient lifting combined with high-stakes decision-making under sleep deprivation create compounded fatigue risk. In-station naps during quiet periods are normal and should be protected.
avoid_patterns: Skipping in-station nap opportunities during 24h shifts
Caffeine within 5 hours of off-duty sleep window
Driving home after a 24h shift without a nap or handoff
recovery_notes: The 48h off period after a 24h shift is a full recovery window — the first 24h should be treated as protected sleep/recovery. Social obligations in this window should be deferred. Strategic use of in-station naps (10-20 min) during slow periods significantly reduces overall fatigue accumulation.
